title: install --update rewrites an existing catalog-origin empty block file with the starter (unguarded, currently benign)
description: Under --update the block branch overwrites a catalog-origin block file that exists on disk but holds no items with the catalog's empty starter, with no short-circuit for the already-present-and-equal case. No data is lost today (empty -> empty), but the rewrite is unguarded and runs on every update.

evidence.reference: the block branch overwrites an existing empty catalog-origin block with the starter under --update rather than skipping an already-equal file

impact: Benign today (no item data lost). Filed for completeness of the update path's clobber semantics and to make the update idempotent at the file level — an unguarded rewrite of an on-disk file is a latent surface for loss if empty blocks ever carry non-item content.
canonical_vocabulary: idempotent skip of an unchanged on-disk block
proposed_resolution: Skip rewriting an existing catalog-origin block whose content already equals the starter; delivered within the unified update command (FEAT-006).
